Interface RedisListCommands

All Known Subinterfaces:
DefaultedRedisClusterConnection, DefaultedRedisConnection, RedisClusterConnection, RedisCommands, RedisConnection, RedisConnectionUtils.RedisConnectionProxy, StringRedisConnection
All Known Implementing Classes:
AbstractRedisConnection, DefaultStringRedisConnection, JedisClusterConnection, JedisConnection, LettuceClusterConnection, LettuceConnection
@NullUnmarked public interface RedisListCommands
List-specific commands supported by Redis.
Author:
Costin Leau, Christoph Strobl, Mark Paluch, dengliming
See Also:
  • RedisCommands

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested Classes
    Modifier and Type
    Interface
    Description
    static enum 
    RedisListCommands.Direction
    List move direction.
    static enum 
    RedisListCommands.Position
    List insertion position.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    byte[]
    bLMove(byte @NonNull [] sourceKey, byte @NonNull [] destinationKey, @NonNull RedisListCommands.Direction from, @NonNull RedisListCommands.Direction to, double timeout)
    Atomically returns and removes the first/last element (head/tail depending on the from argument) of the list stored at sourceKey, and pushes the element at the first/last element (head/tail depending on the to argument) of the list stored at destinationKey.
    @Nullable List<byte @NonNull []>
    bLPop(int timeout, byte @NonNull [] @NonNull ... keys)
    Removes and returns first element from lists stored at keys.
    List<byte @NonNull []>
    bRPop(int timeout, byte @NonNull [] @NonNull ... keys)
    Removes and returns last element from lists stored at keys.
    byte[]
    bRPopLPush(int timeout, byte @NonNull [] srcKey, byte @NonNull [] dstKey)
    Remove the last element from list at srcKey, append it to dstKey and return its value.
    byte[]
    lIndex(byte @NonNull [] key, long index)
    Get element at index form list at key.
    Long
    lInsert(byte @NonNull [] key, @NonNull RedisListCommands.Position where, byte @NonNull [] pivot, byte @NonNull [] value)
    Insert value RedisListCommands.Position.BEFORE or RedisListCommands.Position.AFTER existing pivot for key.
    Long
    lLen(byte @NonNull [] key)
    Get the size of list stored at key.
    byte[]
    lMove(byte @NonNull [] sourceKey, byte @NonNull [] destinationKey, @NonNull RedisListCommands.Direction from, @NonNull RedisListCommands.Direction to)
    Atomically returns and removes the first/last element (head/tail depending on the from argument) of the list stored at sourceKey, and pushes the element at the first/last element (head/tail depending on the to argument) of the list stored at destinationKey.
    byte[]
    lPop(byte @NonNull [] key)
    Removes and returns first element in list stored at key.
    List<byte @NonNull []>
    lPop(byte @NonNull [] key, long count)
    Removes and returns first elements in list stored at key.
    default Long
    lPos(byte @NonNull [] key, byte @NonNull [] element)
    Returns the index of matching elements inside the list stored at given key.
    List<Long>
    lPos(byte @NonNull [] key, byte @NonNull [] element, @Nullable Integer rank, @Nullable Integer count)
    Returns the index of matching elements inside the list stored at given key.
    Long
    lPush(byte @NonNull [] key, byte @NonNull [] @NonNull ... values)
    Prepend values to key.
    Long
    lPushX(byte @NonNull [] key, byte @NonNull [] value)
    Prepend values to key only if the list exists.
    List<byte @NonNull []>
    lRange(byte @NonNull [] key, long start, long end)
    Get elements between start and end from list at key.
    Long
    lRem(byte @NonNull [] key, long count, byte @NonNull [] value)
    Removes the first count occurrences of value from the list stored at key.
    void
    lSet(byte @NonNull [] key, long index, byte @NonNull [] value)
    Set the value list element at index.
    void
    lTrim(byte @NonNull [] key, long start, long end)
    Trim list at key to elements between start and end.
    byte[]
    rPop(byte @NonNull [] key)
    Removes and returns last element in list stored at key.
    List<byte @NonNull []>
    rPop(byte @NonNull [] key, long count)
    Removes and returns last elements in list stored at key.
    byte[]
    rPopLPush(byte @NonNull [] srcKey, byte @NonNull [] dstKey)
    Remove the last element from list at srcKey, append it to dstKey and return its value.
    Long
    rPush(byte @NonNull [] key, byte @NonNull [] @NonNull ... values)
    Append values to key.
    Long
    rPushX(byte @NonNull [] key, byte @NonNull [] value)
    Append values to key only if the list exists.

  • Method Details

    • rPush

      Long rPush(byte @NonNull [] key, byte @NonNull [] @NonNull ... values)
      Append values to key.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      values - must not be empty.
      Returns:
      null when used in pipeline / transaction.
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: RPUSH

    • lPos

      default Long lPos(byte @NonNull [] key, byte @NonNull [] element)
      Returns the index of matching elements inside the list stored at given key.
      Requires Redis 6.0.6 or newer.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      element - must not be null.
      Returns:
      null when used in pipeline / transaction.
      Since:
      2.4
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: LPOS

    • lPos

      List<Long> lPos(byte @NonNull [] key, byte @NonNull [] element, @Nullable Integer rank, @Nullable Integer count)
      Returns the index of matching elements inside the list stored at given key.
      Requires Redis 6.0.6 or newer.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      element - must not be null.
      rank - specifies the "rank" of the first element to return, in case there are multiple matches. A rank of 1 means to return the first match, 2 to return the second match, and so forth.
      count - number of matches to return.
      Returns:
      null when used in pipeline / transaction.
      Since:
      2.4
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: LPOS

    • lPush

      Long lPush(byte @NonNull [] key, byte @NonNull [] @NonNull ... values)
      Prepend values to key.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      values - must not be empty.
      Returns:
      null when used in pipeline / transaction.
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: LPUSH

    • rPushX

      Long rPushX(byte @NonNull [] key, byte @NonNull [] value)
      Append values to key only if the list exists.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      value - must not be null.
      Returns:
      null when used in pipeline / transaction.
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: RPUSHX

    • lPushX

      Long lPushX(byte @NonNull [] key, byte @NonNull [] value)
      Prepend values to key only if the list exists.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      value - must not be null.
      Returns:
      null when used in pipeline / transaction.
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: LPUSHX

    • lLen

      Long lLen(byte @NonNull [] key)
      Get the size of list stored at key.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      Returns:
      null when used in pipeline / transaction.
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: LLEN

    • lRange

      List<byte @NonNull []> lRange(byte @NonNull [] key, long start, long end)
      Get elements between start and end from list at key.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      start -
      end -
      Returns:
      empty List if key does not exists or range does not contain values. null when used in pipeline / transaction.
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: LRANGE

    • lTrim

      void lTrim(byte @NonNull [] key, long start, long end)
      Trim list at key to elements between start and end.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      start -
      end -
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: LTRIM

    • lIndex

      byte[] lIndex(byte @NonNull [] key, long index)
      Get element at index form list at key.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      index - zero based index value. Use negative number to designate elements starting at the tail.
      Returns:
      null when index is out of range or when used in pipeline / transaction.
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: LINDEX

    • lInsert

      Long lInsert(byte @NonNull [] key, @NonNull RedisListCommands.Position where, byte @NonNull [] pivot, byte @NonNull [] value)
      Insert value RedisListCommands.Position.BEFORE or RedisListCommands.Position.AFTER existing pivot for key.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      where - must not be null.
      pivot - must not be null.
      value - must not be null.
      Returns:
      null when used in pipeline / transaction.
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: LINSERT

    • lMove

      byte[] lMove(byte @NonNull [] sourceKey, byte @NonNull [] destinationKey, @NonNull RedisListCommands.Direction from, @NonNull RedisListCommands.Direction to)
      Atomically returns and removes the first/last element (head/tail depending on the from argument) of the list stored at sourceKey, and pushes the element at the first/last element (head/tail depending on the to argument) of the list stored at destinationKey.
      Parameters:
      sourceKey - must not be null.
      destinationKey - must not be null.
      from - must not be null.
      to - must not be null.
      Returns:
      null when used in pipeline / transaction.
      Since:
      2.6
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: LMOVE
      • bLMove(byte[], byte[], Direction, Direction, double)

    • bLMove

      byte[] bLMove(byte @NonNull [] sourceKey, byte @NonNull [] destinationKey, @NonNull RedisListCommands.Direction from, @NonNull RedisListCommands.Direction to, double timeout)
      Atomically returns and removes the first/last element (head/tail depending on the from argument) of the list stored at sourceKey, and pushes the element at the first/last element (head/tail depending on the to argument) of the list stored at destinationKey.

      Blocks connection until element available or timeout reached.

      Parameters:
      sourceKey - must not be null.
      destinationKey - must not be null.
      from - must not be null.
      to - must not be null.
      timeout -
      Returns:
      null when used in pipeline / transaction.
      Since:
      2.6
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: BLMOVE
      • lMove(byte[], byte[], Direction, Direction)

    • lSet

      void lSet(byte @NonNull [] key, long index, byte @NonNull [] value)
      Set the value list element at index.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      index -
      value -
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: LSET

    • lRem

      Long lRem(byte @NonNull [] key, long count, byte @NonNull [] value)
      Removes the first count occurrences of value from the list stored at key.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      count -
      value -
      Returns:
      null when used in pipeline / transaction.
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: LREM

    • lPop

      byte[] lPop(byte @NonNull [] key)
      Removes and returns first element in list stored at key.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      Returns:
      null when key does not exist or used in pipeline / transaction.
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: LPOP

    • lPop

      List<byte @NonNull []> lPop(byte @NonNull [] key, long count)
      Removes and returns first elements in list stored at key.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      count -
      Returns:
      null when key does not exist or used in pipeline / transaction.
      Since:
      2.6
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: LPOP

    • rPop

      byte[] rPop(byte @NonNull [] key)
      Removes and returns last element in list stored at key.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      Returns:
      null when key does not exist or used in pipeline / transaction.
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: RPOP

    • rPop

      List<byte @NonNull []> rPop(byte @NonNull [] key, long count)
      Removes and returns last elements in list stored at key.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      count -
      Returns:
      null when key does not exist or used in pipeline / transaction.
      Since:
      2.6
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: RPOP

    • bLPop

      @Nullable List<byte @NonNull []> bLPop(int timeout, byte @NonNull [] @NonNull ... keys)
      Removes and returns first element from lists stored at keys.
      Blocks connection until element available or timeout reached.
      Parameters:
      timeout - seconds to block.
      keys - must not be null.
      Returns:
      empty List when no element could be popped and the timeout was reached. null when used in pipeline / transaction.
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: BLPOP
      • lPop(byte[])

    • bRPop

      List<byte @NonNull []> bRPop(int timeout, byte @NonNull [] @NonNull ... keys)
      Removes and returns last element from lists stored at keys.
      Blocks connection until element available or timeout reached.
      Parameters:
      timeout - seconds to block.
      keys - must not be null.
      Returns:
      empty List when no element could be popped and the timeout was reached. null when used in pipeline / transaction.
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: BRPOP
      • rPop(byte[])

    • rPopLPush

      byte[] rPopLPush(byte @NonNull [] srcKey, byte @NonNull [] dstKey)
      Remove the last element from list at srcKey, append it to dstKey and return its value.
      Parameters:
      srcKey - must not be null.
      dstKey - must not be null.
      Returns:
      can be null.
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: RPOPLPUSH

    • bRPopLPush

      byte[] bRPopLPush(int timeout, byte @NonNull [] srcKey, byte @NonNull [] dstKey)
      Remove the last element from list at srcKey, append it to dstKey and return its value.
      Blocks connection until element available or timeout reached.
      Parameters:
      timeout - seconds to block.
      srcKey - must not be null.
      dstKey - must not be null.
      Returns:
      can be null.
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: BRPOPLPUSH
      • rPopLPush(byte[], byte[])
